// MB\n  gpu: number | null; // GPU time in ms (may not be available)\n  drawCalls: number;\n  triangles: number;\n  event: PerfEvent;\n}\n\n/**\n * Session statistics returned by __PERF_STOP_SESSION__\n */\ninterface PerfSessionStats {\n  name: string;\n  duration: number; // seconds\n  totalFrames: number;\n  fps: {\n    avg: number;\n    min: number;\n    max: number;\n    p1: number; // 1st percentile (worst 1%)\n    p99: number; // 99th percentile (best 99%)\n  };\n  ms: {\n    avg: number;\n    max: number;\n  };\n  framesBelow30: number;\n  framesBelow60: number;\n  entries: PerfLogEntry[];\n}\n\n/**\n * Active session state\n */\ninterface PerfSession {\n  name: string;\n  startTime: number;\n  entries: PerfLogEntry[];\n  fpsValues: number[];\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// CONSTANTS\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nconst LOG_BUFFER_DURATION_MS = 60000; // 60 seconds of logs\nconst LOG_INTERVAL_FRAMES = 60; // Log every 60 frames (~1 second at 60fps)\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// GLOBAL STATE (accessible via window)\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n// Circular buffer for performance logs\nlet perfLogBuffer: PerfLogEntry[] = [];\nlet currentSession: PerfSession | null = null;\nlet frameCounter = 0;\nlet fpsHistory: number[] = [];\nlet msHistory: number[] = [];\n\n// Rolling statistics for averaging\nconst rollingWindow = 60; // frames for rolling average\n\n/**\n * Clear old entries from buffer based on timestamp\n */\nfunction pruneOldEntries() {\n  const cutoff = Date.now() - LOG_BUFFER_DURATION_MS;\n  perfLogBuffer = perfLogBuffer.filter((entry) => entry.timestamp > cutoff);\n}\n\n/**\n * Calculate percentile from sorted array\n */\nfunction percentile(sortedArr: number[], p: number): number {\n  if (sortedArr.length === 0) return 0;\n  const index = Math.ceil((p / 100) * sortedArr.length) - 1;\n  return sortedArr[Math.max(0, Math.min(index, sortedArr.length - 1))];\n}\n\n/**\n * Export the global performance API to window\n */\nfunction setupGlobalAPI() {\n  // Make log buffer accessible\n  Object.defineProperty(window, '__PERF_LOG__', {\n    get: () => [...perfLogBuffer],\n    configurable: true,\n  });\n\n  // Export full log\n  (window as any).__PERF_EXPORT__ = (): PerfLogEntry[] => {\n    return [...perfLogBuffer];\n  };\n\n  // Start a named session\n  (window as any).__PERF_START_SESSION__ = (name: string): void => {\n    if (currentSession) {\n      console.warn(`[PERF] Session \"${currentSession.name}\" already running. Stopping it first.`);\n      (window as any).__PERF_STOP_SESSION__();\n    }\n    currentSession = {\n      name,\n      startTime: Date.now(),\n      entries: [],\n      fpsValues: [],\n    };\n    console.log(`[PERF] Session \"${name}\" started`);\n  };\n\n  // Stop current session and return stats\n  (window as any).__PERF_STOP_SESSION__ = (): PerfSessionStats | null => {\n    if (!currentSession) {\n      console.warn('[PERF] No active session to stop');\n      return null;\n    }\n\n    const session = currentSession;\n    currentSession = null;\n\n    const duration = (Date.now() - session.startTime) / 1000;\n    const sortedFps = [...session.fpsValues].sort((a, b) => a - b);\n\n    const stats: PerfSessionStats = {\n      name: session.name,\n      duration,\n      totalFrames: session.fpsValues.length,\n      fps: {\n        avg: sortedFps.length > 0 ? sortedFps.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / sortedFps.length : 0,\n        min: sortedFps.length > 0 ? sortedFps[0] : 0,\n        max: sortedFps.length > 0 ? sortedFps[sortedFps.length - 1] : 0,\n        p1: percentile(sortedFps, 1),\n        p99: percentile(sortedFps, 99),\n      },\n      ms: {\n        avg: session.entries.length > 0\n          ? session.entries.reduce((a, e) => a + e.ms.current, 0) / session.entries.length\n          : 0,\n        max: session.entries.length > 0\n          ? Math.max(...session.entries.map((e) => e.ms.current))\n          : 0,\n      },\n      framesBelow30: session.fpsValues.filter((fps) => fps < 30).length,\n      framesBelow60: session.fpsValues.filter((fps) => fps < 60).length,\n      entries: session.entries,\n    };\n\n    console.log(`[PERF] Session \"${session.name}\" stopped:`, {\n      duration: `${duration.toFixed(2)}s`,\n      avgFPS: stats.fps.avg.toFixed(1),\n      p1FPS: stats.fps.p1.toFixed(1),\n      p99FPS: stats.fps.p99.toFixed(1),\n      framesBelow30: stats.framesBelow30,\n      framesBelow60: stats.framesBelow60,\n    });\n\n    return stats;\n  };\n\n  // Get current session status\n  (window as any).__PERF_SESSION_STATUS__ = (): { active: boolean; name?: string; duration?: number } => {\n    if (!currentSession) {\n      return { active: false };\n    }\n    return {\n      active: true,\n      name: currentSession.name,\n      duration: (Date.now() - currentSession.startTime) / 1000,\n    };\n  };\n\n  // Clear log buffer\n  (window as any).__PERF_CLEAR__ = (): void => {\n    perfLogBuffer = [];\n    console.log('[PERF] Log buffer cleared');\n  };\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// PERF LOGGER COMPONENT (runs inside R3F Canvas)\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/**\n * Internal component that reads r3f-perf data and logs it\n * Must be rendered inside R3F Canvas context\n */\nfunction PerfLogger({ currentEvent }: { currentEvent: PerfEvent }) {\n  const perfData = usePerf();\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    if (!perfData) return;\n\n    frameCounter++;\n\n    // Track FPS for rolling average\n    const currentFps = perfData.log?.fps ?? 0;\n    const currentMs = perfData.log?.ms ?? 16.67;\n\n    fpsHistory.push(currentFps);\n    msHistory.push(currentMs);\n\n    // Keep rolling window\n    if (fpsHistory.length > rollingWindow) {\n      fpsHistory.shift();\n      msHistory.shift();\n    }\n\n    // Track for active session (every frame)\n    if (currentSession) {\n      currentSession.fpsValues.push(currentFps);\n    }\n\n    // Log every N frames\n    if (frameCounter >= LOG_INTERVAL_FRAMES) {\n      frameCounter = 0;\n\n      // Calculate rolling stats\n      const avgFps = fpsHistory.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / fpsHistory.length;\n      const minFps = Math.min(...fpsHistory);\n      const maxFps = Math.max(...fpsHistory);\n      const avgMs = msHistory.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / msHistory.length;\n      const maxMs = Math.max(...msHistory);\n\n      const entry: PerfLogEntry = {\n        timestamp: Date.now(),\n        fps: {\n          current: currentFps,\n          avg: avgFps,\n          min: minFps,\n          max: maxFps,\n        },\n        ms: {\n          current: currentMs,\n          avg: avgMs,\n          max: maxMs,\n        },\n        memory: perfData.log?.mem ?? 0,\n        gpu: perfData.log?.gpu ?? null,\n        drawCalls: perfData.log?.calls ?? 0,\n        triangles: perfData.log?.triangles ?? 0,\n        event: currentEvent,\n      };\n\n      // Add to buffer\n      perfLogBuffer.push(entry);\n      pruneOldEntries();\n\n      // Add to active session\n      if (currentSession) {\n        currentSession.entries.push(entry);\n      }\n    }\n  });\n\n  return null;\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// MAIN PERF MONITOR COMPONENT\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\ninterface PerfMonitorProps {\n  /** Whether the perf panel is visible */\n  visible?: boolean;\n  /** Callback when visibility changes (for external control) */\n  onVisibilityChange?: (visible: boolean) => void;\n  /** Current event type for logging context */\n  currentEvent?: PerfEvent;\n  /** Position of the panel */\n  position?: 'top-left' | 'top-right' | 'bottom-left' | 'bottom-right';\n}\n\n/**\n * Performance monitor component with r3f-perf panel and logging system\n *\n * This component should be rendered INSIDE the R3F Canvas.\n *\n * @example\n * <Canvas>\n *   <PerfMonitor visible={showPerf} />\n *   {... other scene content ...}\n * </Canvas>\n */\nexport function PerfMonitor({\n  visible = true,\n  currentEvent = 'idle',\n  position = 'bottom-left', // BUG-006: Changed from top-right to avoid blocking navbar controls\n}: PerfMonitorProps) {\n  // Setup global API on mount\n  useEffect(() => {\n    setupGlobalAPI();\n    console.log('[PERF] Performance monitor initialized. Available commands:');\n    console.log('  window.__PERF_LOG__ - Access log buffer');\n    console.log('  window.__PERF_EXPORT__() - Export full log');\n    console.log('  window.__PERF_START_SESSION__(name) - Start recording');\n    console.log('  window.__PERF_STOP_SESSION__() - Stop and get stats');\n    console.log('  window.__PERF_CLEAR__() - Clear log buffer');\n\n    return () => {\n      // Cleanup (optional - keep API available for debugging)\n    };\n  }, []);\n\n  return (\n    <>\n      {/* r3f-perf visual panel */}\n      {visible && (\n        <Perf\n          position={position}\n          minimal={false}\n          showGraph={true}\n          colorBlind={false}\n          antialias={true}\n          overClock={true}\n          deepAnalyze={true}\n          matrixUpdate={true}\n        />\n      )}\n\n      {/* Logger that runs continuously (even when panel is hidden) */}\n      <PerfLogger currentEvent={currentEvent} />\n    </>\n  );\n}\n\n/**\n * Hook to toggle perf monitor visibility with keyboard shortcut\n *\n * @example\n * const { visible, toggle } = usePerfMonitorToggle();\n */\nexport function usePerfMonitorToggle(initialVisible = false) {\n  const [visible, setVisible] = useState(initialVisible);\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    const handleKeyDown = (e: KeyboardEvent) => {\n      // Toggle on 'P' key (not when typing in input)\n      if (\n        e.key === 'p' &&\n        !e.ctrlKey &&\n        !e.metaKey &&\n        !e.altKey &&\n        !(e.target instanceof HTMLInputElement) &&\n        !(e.target instanceof HTMLTextAreaElement)\n      ) {\n        setVisible((v) => !v);\n      }\n    };\n\n    window.addEventListener('keydown', handleKeyDown);\n    return () => window.removeEventListener('keydown', handleKeyDown);\n  }, []);\n\n  const toggle = useCallback(() => setVisible((v) => !v), []);\n\n  return { visible, toggle, setVisible };\n}\n\n// Export types for external use\nexport type { PerfEvent, PerfLogEntry, PerfSessionStats };\n","numLines":406,"startLine":1,"totalLines":406}},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"2d79d884-8985-4e39-abca-ff39404bfc99"}

'year' | 'month' | 'week' | 'day';\n\nfunction getGranularity(zoom: number): Granularity {\n  if (zoom < 0.5) return 'year';\n  if (zoom < 1.5) return 'month';\n  if (zoom < 3.0) return 'week';\n  return 'day';\n}\n\n/**\n * Get the minimum pixel spacing between labels for each granularity\n */\nfunction getMinLabelSpacing(granularity: Granularity): number {\n  switch (granularity) {\n    case 'year':\n      return 80;\n    case 'month':\n      return 70;\n    case 'week':\n      return 60;\n    case 'day':\n      return 50;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Convert world X coordinate to day offset from start date\n */\nfunction worldXToDayOffset(worldX: number): number {\n  return Math.floor(worldX / pxPerDay);\n}\n\n/**\n * Convert day offset to world X coordinate\n */\nfunction dayOffsetToWorldX(dayOffset: number): number {\n  return dayOffset * pxPerDay;\n}\n\n// D3-MIGRATE-001: Time interval map for granularity-based operations\nconst timeIntervals = {\n  year: timeYear,\n  month: timeMonth,\n  week: timeWeek,\n  day: timeDay,\n} as const;\n\n/**\n * Add days to a date (D3-MIGRATE-001: uses d3-time)\n */\nfunction addDays(date: Date, days: number): Date {\n  return timeDay.offset(date, days);\n}\n\n/**\n * Get the start of a time unit (D3-MIGRATE-001: uses d3-time floor)\n */\nfunction getUnitStart(date: Date, granularity: Granularity): Date {\n  return timeIntervals[granularity].floor(date);\n}\n\n/**\n * Get days until next unit\n */\nfunction getDaysPerUnit(granularity: Granularity): number {\n  switch (granularity) {\n    case 'year':\n      return 365;\n    case 'month':\n      return 30;\n    case 'week':\n      return 7;\n    case 'day':\n      return 1;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Advance to next unit (D3-MIGRATE-001: uses d3-time offset)\n */\nfunction advanceUnit(date: Date, granularity: Granularity): Date {\n  return timeIntervals[granularity].offset(date, 1);\n}\n\n/**\n * Format date based on granularity\n */\nfunction formatDate(date: Date, granularity: Granularity): string {\n  switch (granularity) {\n    case 'year':\n      return formatYear(date);\n    case 'month':\n      return formatMonth(date);\n    case 'week':\n      return formatWeek(date);\n    case 'day':\n      return formatDay(date);\n  }\n}\n\ninterface TimeLabel {\n  dayOffset: number;\n  worldX: number;\n  label: string;\n  isMinor: boolean; // For smaller tick marks\n}\n\n/**\n * Generate labels for the visible viewport\n */\nfunction generateLabels(\n  viewportLeftX: number,\n  viewportRightX: number,\n  granularity: Granularity\n): TimeLabel[] {\n  const labels: TimeLabel[] = [];\n\n  // Add padding to viewport to show labels near edges\n  const padding = getDaysPerUnit(granularity) * pxPerDay * 2;\n  const startDayOffset = worldXToDayOffset(viewportLeftX - padding);\n  const endDayOffset = worldXToDayOffset(viewportRightX + padding);\n\n  // Convert to dates\n  const startDate = addDays(START_DATE, startDayOffset);\n  const endDate = addDays(START_DATE, endDayOffset);\n\n  // Find the first unit boundary at or before startDate\n  let currentDate = getUnitStart(startDate, granularity);\n\n  // If currentDate is before START_DATE, advance to START_DATE\n  if (currentDate < START_DATE) {\n    currentDate = getUnitStart(START_DATE, granularity);\n    if (currentDate < START_DATE) {\n      currentDate = advanceUnit(currentDate, granularity);\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Generate labels until we pass endDate\n  while (currentDate <= endDate) {\n    const dayOffset = Math.round(\n      (currentDate.getTime() - START_DATE.getTime()) / (1000 * 60 * 60 * 24)\n    );\n    const worldX = dayOffsetToWorldX(dayOffset);\n\n    // Only add labels within reasonable range\n    if (worldX >= viewportLeftX - padding && worldX <= viewportRightX + padding) {\n      labels.push({\n        dayOffset,\n        worldX,\n        label: formatDate(currentDate, granularity),\n        isMinor: false,\n      });\n    }\n\n    currentDate = advanceUnit(currentDate, granularity);\n  }\n\n  return labels;\n}\n\nexport function TimelineHeader({\n  viewportLeftX,\n  viewportRightX,\n  zoom,\n  sidebarWidth,\n  onDateClick,\n}: TimelineHeaderProps) {\n  const granularity = useMemo(() => getGranularity(zoom), [zoom]);\n\n  const labels = useMemo(\n    () => generateLabels(viewportLeftX, viewportRightX, granularity),\n    [viewportLeftX, viewportRightX, granularity]\n  );\n\n  // Calculate viewport width with defensive check\n  const viewportWidth = viewportRightX - viewportLeftX;\n\n  // BUG-005: Guard against invalid viewport (e.g., during initialization)\n  const isViewportValid = isFinite(viewportWidth) && viewportWidth > 0;\n\n  return (\n    <div\n      style={{\n        height: 32,\n        backgroundColor: '#f5f8fa',\n        borderBottom: '1px solid #e1e8ed',\n        display: 'flex',\n        alignItems: 'center',\n        overflow: 'hidden',\n        position: 'relative',\n        marginLeft: sidebarWidth,\n      }}\n    >\n      {isViewportValid && labels.map((label, index) => {\n        // Calculate position as percentage of viewport\n        const positionInViewport = label.worldX - viewportLeftX;\n        const percentPosition = (positionInViewport / viewportWidth) * 100;\n\n        // BUG-005: Skip labels that would be too far off screen\n        // Use wider bounds for year granularity since labels are spaced far apart\n        const skipMargin = granularity === 'year' ? 50 : 20;\n        if (!isFinite(percentPosition) || percentPosition < -skipMargin || percentPosition > 100 + skipMargin) {\n          return null;\n        }\n\n        // BUG-005: Adjust transform for edge labels to keep them visible\n        // At edges, don't center the label - align it to stay within bounds\n        let transformX = '-50%';  // Default: center on position\n        if (percentPosition < 5) {\n          // Near left edge: align left edge to position\n          transformX = '0%';\n        } else if (percentPosition > 95) {\n          // Near right edge: align right edge to position\n          transformX = '-100%';\n        }\n\n        return (\n          <div\n            key={`${label.label}-${index}`}\n            onClick={() => onDateClick?.(label.dayOffset)}\n            style={{\n              position: 'absolute',\n              left: `${percentPosition}%`,\n `Click to navigate to ${label.label}` : undefined}\n          >\n            <span>{label.label}</span>\n            <div\n              style={{\n                width: 1,\n                height: 6,\n                backgroundColor: '#d8e1e8',\n                marginTop: 2,\n              }}\n            />\n          </div>\n        );\n      })}\n\n      {/* Gradient overlays to fade labels at edges - z-index 1 so labels (z-index 2) are above */}\n      <div\n        style={{\n          position: 'absolute',\n          left: 0,\n          top: 0,\n          bottom: 0,\n          width: 20, // BUG-005: Reduced from 30 to leave more space for labels\n          background: 'linear-gradient(to right, #f5f8fa, transparent)',\n          pointerEvents: 'none',\n          zIndex: 1,\n        }}\n      />\n      <div\n        style={{\n          position: 'absolute',\n          right: 0,\n          top: 0,\n          bottom: 0,\n          width: 20, // BUG-005: Reduced from 30 to leave more space for labels\n          background: 'linear-gradient(to left, #f5f8fa, transparent)',\n          pointerEvents: 'none',\n          zIndex: 1,\n        }}\n      />\n    </div>\n  );\n}\n","numLines":337,"startLine":1,"totalLines":337}},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"8bff573a-8e72-45d9-bcd1-ba1eef199306"}

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\ninterface StatsMonitorProps {\n  /** Whether the stats panel is visible */\n  visible?: boolean;\n  /** Position of the panel */\n containerRef.current.appendChild(dom);\n\n    // Animation loop for stats update\n    let lastTime = performance.now();\n    const animate = () => {\n      const currentTime = performance.now();\n      // Only update stats if enough time has passed (throttle to ~60fps)\n      if (currentTime - lastTime >= 16) {\n        stats.update();\n        lastTime = currentTime;\n      }\n ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\ninterface PerformancePanelProps {\n  visible?: boolean;\n  position?: 'top-left' | 'top-right' | 'bottom-left' | 'bottom-right';\n  /** Custom metrics to display */\n  customMetrics?: Record<string, number | string>;\n}\n\n/**\n * Combined performance panel with FPS + custom metrics\n */\nexport function PerformancePanel({\n  visible = true,\n  position = 'top-left',\n  customMetrics = {}\n}: PerformancePanelProps) {\n  const containerRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);\n  const statsRef = useRef<Stats | null>(null);\n  const frameIdRef = useRef<number>(0);\n  const [fps, setFps] = useState(0);\n  const [ms, setMs] = useState(0);\n  const fpsHistoryRef = useRef<number[]>([]);\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    if (!visible) return;\n\n    const stats = new Stats();\n    stats.showPanel(0);\n    statsRef.current = stats;\n\n    let lastTime = performance.now();\n    let frames = 0;\n\n    const animate = () => {\n      const currentTime = performance.now();\n      frames++;\n\n      // Update FPS every second\n      if (currentTime - lastTime >= 1000) {\n        const currentFps = Math.round(frames * 1000 / (currentTime - lastTime));\n        const currentMs = Math.round((currentTime - lastTime) / frames * 100) / 100;\n\n        setFps(currentFps);\n        setMs(currentMs);\n\n        // Keep FPS new Date(start) : start;\n  const e = typeof end === 'string' ? new Date(end) : end;\n  const diffTime = e.getTime() - s.getTime();\n  return Math.floor(diffTime / (1000 * 60 * 60 * 24));\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// DATA GENERATION\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/**\n * Generate train entities\n */\nfunction generateTrains(count: number): Train[] {\n  return Array.from({ length: count }, (_, i) => {\n    const isAlstom = i < Math.floor(count * 0.6); // 60% Alstom\n    return {\n      id: `TRAIN-${String(i + 1).padStart(3, '0')}`,\n      name: `Train ${i + 1}`,\n      assetType: (isAlstom ? predecessor\n * - Predecessor must end before the current task starts (valid dependency)\n * - Mix of same-train and cross-train dependencies\n * - Overhauls often depend on inspections (realistic pattern)\n */\nfunction addDependencies(tasks: Task[], meta: DatasetMeta): void {\n  // Build a lookup by start date for efficient predecessor search\n  const sortedTasks = [...tasks].sort(\n    (a, (task.hasConflict) continue;\n\n    // ~12% chance of having a dependency\n    if (Math.random() >= 0.12) continue;\n\n    const taskStartTime = new Date(task.startDate).getTime();\n\n    // 70% same-train dependency, 30% cross-train dependency\n    const sameTrain = Math.random() < 0.7;\n\n    let candidates: Task[];\n    if (sameTrain) {\n      // Find tasks on the same train that end before this one starts\n      candidates = (tasksByTrain.get(task.trainId) || []).filter((t) => {\n        const endTime = new Date(t.endDate).getTime();\n        // Predecessor must end at least 1 && gap <= 30; // Tighter window for cross-train\n      });\n    }\n\n    if (candidates.length > 0) {\n      // Pick a random predecessor from candidates\n      const predecessor = candidates[Math.floor(Math.random() * candidates.length)];\n      task.predecessorId = predecessor.id;\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Generate complete dataset\n */\nexport function generateData(meta: DatasetMeta = DEFAULT_DATASET_META): GeneratedData {\n  const trains = generateTrains(meta.trainCount);\n  const tasks = generateTasks(trains, meta);\n\n  return { trains, tasks, meta };\n}\n\n// prepareRenderData(data: GeneratedData): {\n  trains: Train[];\n  tasks: TaskRender[];\n} {\n  // Create train index lookup map\n  const trainIndexMap = new Map<string, number>(\n    data.trains.map((t, i) => [t.id, i])\n  );\n\n  // Transform tasks to render format\n  const tasks: TaskRender[] = data.tasks.map((task) => {\n    const trainIndex = trainIndexMap.get(task.trainId);\n component for WebGL/R3F applications\n *\n * Features:\n * - Real-time FPS, MS, Memory, GPU, Draw Calls, Triangles display via r3f-perf\n * - Detailed logging system with circular buffer (60s of metrics)\n * - Session recording for performance testing\n * - Global API exposed via window for programmatic access\n *\n * Usage:\n * - Visual: Press 'P' key or use toggle button to show/hide panel\n * - Console: window.__PERF_LOG__ - access log buffer\n * - Console: window.__PERF_EXPORT__() - export full log as array\n * - Console: window.__PERF_START_SESSION__('test') - start recording session\n * - Console: window.__PERF_STOP_SESSION__() - stop and get session stats\n *\n * @see docs/PERFORMANCE_LOGGING.md for detailed documentation\n */\n\nimport { useEffect, useRef, useCallback, useState } from 'react';\nimport { Perf, usePerf } from 'r3f-perf';\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// TYPES\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/**\n * Event type for performance logging\n */\ntype PerfEvent = 'idle' | 'panning' | 'zooming' | 'animating' | 'lod_transition' | 'unknown';\n\n/**\n * Single metric entry in the log buffer\n */\ninterface PerfLogEntry {\n  timestamp: number;\n  fps: {\n    current: number;\n    avg: number;\n    min: number;\n    max: number;\n  };\n  ms: {\n    current: number;\n    avg: number;\n    max: number;\n  };\n  memory: number; // MB\n  gpu: number | null; // GPU time in ms (may not be available)\n  drawCalls: number;\n  triangles: number;\n  event: PerfEvent;\n}\n\n/**\n * Session statistics returned by __PERF_STOP_SESSION__\n */\ninterface PerfSessionStats {\n  name: string;\n  duration: number; // seconds\n  totalFrames: number;\n  fps: {\n    avg: number;\n    min: number;\n    max: number;\n    p1: number; // 1st percentile (worst 1%)\n    p99: number; // 99th percentile (best 99%)\n  };\n  ms: {\n    avg: number;\n    max: number;\n  };\n  framesBelow30: number;\n  framesBelow60: number;\n  entries: PerfLogEntry[];\n}\n\n/**\n * Active session state\n */\ninterface PerfSession {\n  name: string;\n  startTime: number;\n  entries: PerfLogEntry[];\n  fpsValues: number[];\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// CONSTANTS\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nconst LOG_BUFFER_DURATION_MS = 60000; // 60 seconds of logs\nconst LOG_INTERVAL_FRAMES = 60; // Log every 60 frames (~1 second at 60fps)\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// GLOBAL STATE (accessible via window)\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n// Circular buffer for performance logs\nlet perfLogBuffer: PerfLogEntry[] = [];\nlet currentSession: PerfSession | null = null;\nlet frameCounter = 0;\nlet fpsHistory: number[] = [];\nlet msHistory: number[] = [];\n\n// Rolling statistics for averaging\nconst rollingWindow = 60; // frames for rolling average\n\n/**\n * Clear old entries from buffer based on timestamp\n */\nfunction pruneOldEntries() {\n  const cutoff = Date.now() - LOG_BUFFER_DURATION_MS;\n  perfLogBuffer = perfLogBuffer.filter((entry) => entry.timestamp > cutoff);\n}\n\n/**\n * Calculate percentile from sorted array\n */\nfunction percentile(sortedArr: number[], p: number): number {\n  if (sortedArr.length === 0) return 0;\n  const index = Math.ceil((p / 100) * sortedArr.length) - 1;\n  return sortedArr[Math.max(0, Math.min(index, sortedArr.length - 1))];\n}\n\n/**\n * Export the global performance API to window\n */\nfunction setupGlobalAPI() {\n  // Make log buffer accessible\n  Object.defineProperty(window, '__PERF_LOG__', {\n    get: () => [...perfLogBuffer],\n    configurable: true,\n  });\n\n  // Export full log\n  (window as any).__PERF_EXPORT__ = (): PerfLogEntry[] => {\n    return [...perfLogBuffer];\n  };\n\n  // Start a named session\n  (window as any).__PERF_START_SESSION__ = (name: string): void => {\n    if (currentSession) {\n      console.warn(`[PERF] Session \"${currentSession.name}\" already running. Stopping it first.`);\n      (window as any).__PERF_STOP_SESSION__();\n    }\n    currentSession = {\n      name,\n      startTime: Date.now(),\n      entries: [],\n      fpsValues: [],\n    };\n    console.log(`[PERF] Session \"${name}\" started`);\n  };\n\n  // Stop current session and return stats\n  (window as any).__PERF_STOP_SESSION__ = (): PerfSessionStats | null => {\n    if (!currentSession) {\n      console.warn('[PERF] No active session to stop');\n      return null;\n    }\n\n    const session = currentSession;\n    currentSession = null;\n\n    const duration = (Date.now() - session.startTime) / 1000;\n    const sortedFps = [...session.fpsValues].sort((a, b) => a - b);\n\n    const stats: PerfSessionStats = {\n      name: session.name,\n      duration,\n      totalFrames: session.fpsValues.length,\n      fps: {\n        avg: sortedFps.length > 0 ? sortedFps.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / sortedFps.length : 0,\n        min: sortedFps.length > 0 ? sortedFps[0] : 0,\n        max: sortedFps.length > 0 ? sortedFps[sortedFps.length - 1] : 0,\n        p1: percentile(sortedFps, 1),\n        p99: percentile(sortedFps, 99),\n      },\n      ms: {\n        avg: session.entries.length > 0\n          ? session.entries.reduce((a, e) => a + e.ms.current, 0) / session.entries.length\n          : 0,\n        max: session.entries.length > 0\n          ? Math.max(...session.entries.map((e) => e.ms.current))\n          : 0,\n      },\n      framesBelow30: session.fpsValues.filter((fps) => fps < 30).length,\n      framesBelow60: session.fpsValues.filter((fps) => fps < 60).length,\n      entries: session.entries,\n    };\n\n    console.log(`[PERF] Session \"${session.name}\" stopped:`, {\n      duration: `${duration.toFixed(2)}s`,\n      avgFPS: stats.fps.avg.toFixed(1),\n      p1FPS: stats.fps.p1.toFixed(1),\n      p99FPS: stats.fps.p99.toFixed(1),\n      framesBelow30: stats.framesBelow30,\n      framesBelow60: stats.framesBelow60,\n    });\n\n    return stats;\n  };\n\n  // Get current session status\n  (window as any).__PERF_SESSION_STATUS__ = (): { active: boolean; name?: string; duration?: number } => {\n    if (!currentSession) {\n      return { active: false };\n    }\n    return {\n      active: true,\n      name: currentSession.name,\n      duration: (Date.now() - currentSession.startTime) / 1000,\n    };\n  };\n\n  // Clear log buffer\n  (window as any).__PERF_CLEAR__ = (): void => {\n    perfLogBuffer = [];\n    console.log('[PERF] Log buffer cleared');\n  };\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// PERF LOGGER COMPONENT (runs inside R3F Canvas)\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/**\n * Internal component that reads r3f-perf data and logs it\n * Must be rendered inside R3F Canvas context\n */\nfunction PerfLogger({ currentEvent }: { currentEvent: PerfEvent }) {\n  const perfData = usePerf();\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    if (!perfData) return;\n\n    frameCounter++;\n\n    // Track FPS for rolling average\n    const currentFps = perfData.log?.fps ?? 0;\n    const currentMs = perfData.log?.ms ?? 16.67;\n\n    fpsHistory.push(currentFps);\n    msHistory.push(currentMs);\n\n    // Keep rolling window\n    if (fpsHistory.length > rollingWindow) {\n      fpsHistory.shift();\n      msHistory.shift();\n    }\n\n    // Track for active session (every frame)\n    if (currentSession) {\n      currentSession.fpsValues.push(currentFps);\n    }\n\n    // Log every N frames\n    if (frameCounter >= LOG_INTERVAL_FRAMES) {\n      frameCounter = 0;\n\n      // Calculate rolling stats\n      const avgFps = fpsHistory.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / fpsHistory.length;\n      const minFps = Math.min(...fpsHistory);\n      const maxFps = Math.max(...fpsHistory);\n      const avgMs = msHistory.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / msHistory.length;\n      const maxMs = Math.max(...msHistory);\n\n      const entry: PerfLogEntry = {\n        timestamp: Date.now(),\n        fps: {\n          current: currentFps,\n          avg: avgFps,\n          min: minFps,\n          max: maxFps,\n        },\n        ms: {\n          current: currentMs,\n          avg: avgMs,\n          max: maxMs,\n        },\n        memory: perfData.log?.mem ?? 0,\n        gpu: perfData.log?.gpu ?? null,\n        drawCalls: perfData.log?.calls ?? 0,\n        triangles: perfData.log?.triangles ?? 0,\n        event: currentEvent,\n      };\n\n      // Add to buffer\n      perfLogBuffer.push(entry);\n      pruneOldEntries();\n\n      // Add to active session\n      if (currentSession) {\n        currentSession.entries.push(entry);\n      }\n    }\n  });\n\n  return null;\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// MAIN PERF MONITOR COMPONENT\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\ninterface PerfMonitorProps {\n  /** Whether the perf panel is visible */\n  visible?: boolean;\n  /** Callback when visibility changes (for external control) */\n  onVisibilityChange?: (visible: boolean) => void;\n  /** Current event type for logging context */\n  currentEvent?: PerfEvent;\n  /** Position of the panel */\n  position?: 'top-left' | 'top-right' | 'bottom-left' | 'bottom-right';\n}\n\n/**\n * Performance monitor component with r3f-perf panel and logging system\n *\n * This component should be rendered INSIDE the R3F Canvas.\n *\n * @example\n * <Canvas>\n *   <PerfMonitor visible={showPerf} />\n *   {... other scene content ...}\n * </Canvas>\n */\nexport function PerfMonitor({\n  visible = true,\n  currentEvent = 'idle',\n  position = 'bottom-left', // BUG-006: Changed from top-right to avoid blocking navbar controls\n}: PerfMonitorProps) {\n  // Setup global API on mount\n  useEffect(() => {\n    setupGlobalAPI();\n    console.log('[PERF] Performance monitor initialized. Available commands:');\n    console.log('  window.__PERF_LOG__ - Access log buffer');\n    console.log('  window.__PERF_EXPORT__() - Export full log');\n    console.log('  window.__PERF_START_SESSION__(name) - Start recording');\n    console.log('  window.__PERF_STOP_SESSION__() - Stop and get stats');\n    console.log('  window.__PERF_CLEAR__() - Clear log buffer');\n\n    return () => {\n      // Cleanup (optional - keep API available for debugging)\n    };\n  }, []);\n\n  return (\n    <>\n      {/* r3f-perf visual panel */}\n      {visible && (\n        <Perf\n          position={position}\n          minimal={false}\n          showGraph={true}\n          colorBlind={false}\n          antialias={true}\n          overClock={true}\n          deepAnalyze={true}\n          matrixUpdate={true}\n        />\n      )}\n\n      {/* Logger that runs continuously (even when panel is hidden) */}\n      <PerfLogger currentEvent={currentEvent} />\n    </>\n  );\n}\n\n/**\n * Hook to toggle perf monitor visibility with keyboard shortcut\n *\n * @example\n * const { visible, toggle } = usePerfMonitorToggle();\n */\nexport function usePerfMonitorToggle(initialVisible = false) {\n  const [visible, setVisible] = useState(initialVisible);\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    const handleKeyDown = (e: KeyboardEvent) => {\n      // Toggle on 'P' key (not when typing in input)\n      if (\n        e.key === 'p' &&\n        !e.ctrlKey &&\n        !e.metaKey &&\n        !e.altKey &&\n        !(e.target instanceof HTMLInputElement) &&\n        !(e.target instanceof HTMLTextAreaElement)\n      ) {\n        setVisible((v) => !v);\n      }\n    };\n\n    window.addEventListener('keydown', handleKeyDown);\n    return () => window.removeEventListener('keydown', handleKeyDown);\n  }, []);\n\n  const toggle = useCallback(() => setVisible((v) => !v), []);\n\n  return { visible, toggle, setVisible };\n}\n\n// Export types for external use\nexport type { PerfEvent, PerfLogEntry, PerfSessionStats };\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":17,"oldLines":7,"newStart":17,"newLines":7,"lines":["  * @see docs/PERFORMANCE_LOGGING.md Weeks\n *   - Very high zoom (> 3.0): Days\n * - Labels positioned to align with canvas content\n * - Labels don't overlap (spacing adjusted based on zoom)\n *\n * D3-MIGRATE-001: Uses d3-time and d3-time-format for date operations\n */\n\nimport { useMemo } from 'react';\nimport { timeYear, timeMonth, timeWeek, timeDay } from 'd3-time';\nimport { timeFormat } from 'd3-time-format';\nimport { DEFAULT_VIEW_CONFIG, DEFAULT_DATASET_META } from '../data/types';\n\ninterface TimelineHeaderProps {\n  /** Left edge of the visible viewport in world X coordinates */\n  viewportLeftX: number;\n  /** Right edge of the visible viewport in world X coordinates */\n  viewportRightX: number;\n  /** Current zoom level */\n  zoom: number;\n  /** Width of the sidebar (to offset the timeline) */\n  sidebarWidth: number;\n  /** Callback when a date label is clicked (F124: navigation) */\n  onDateClick?: (dayOffset: number) => void;\n}\n\nconst { pxPerDay } = DEFAULT_VIEW_CONFIG;\nconst START_DATE = new Date(DEFAULT_DATASET_META.startDate);\n\n// D3-MIGRATE-001: Format helpers using d3-time-format\nconst formatYear = timeFormat('%Y');\nconst formatMonth = timeFormat('%b %Y');\nconst formatWeek 'day';\n\nfunction getGranularity(zoom: number): Granularity {\n  if (zoom < 0.5) return 'year';\n  if (zoom < 1.5) return 'month';\n  if (zoom < 3.0) return 'week';\n  return 'day';\n}\n\n/**\n * Get the minimum pixel spacing between labels for each granularity\n */\nfunction getMinLabelSpacing(granularity: Granularity): number {\n  switch (granularity) {\n    case 'year':\n      return 80;\n worldXToDayOffset(worldX: number): number {\n  return Math.floor(worldX / pxPerDay);\n}\n\n/**\n * Convert day offset to world X coordinate\n */\nfunction dayOffsetToWorldX(dayOffset: number): number {\n  return dayOffset * pxPerDay;\n}\n\n// D3-MIGRATE-001: Time interval map for granularity-based operations\nconst timeIntervals = {\n  year: timeYear,\n  month: timeMonth,\n  week: timeWeek,\n Get days until next unit\n */\nfunction getDaysPerUnit(granularity: Granularity): number {\n  switch (granularity) {\n    case 'year':\n      return 365;\n    case 'month':\n      return 30;\n    case 'week':\n      return 7;\n    case 'day':\n      return 1;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Advance to next unit (D3-MIGRATE-001: uses d3-time offset)\n */\nfunction advanceUnit(date: Date, granularity: Granularity): Date {\n  return timeIntervals[granularity].offset(date, 1);\n}\n\n/**\n * Format date based on granularity\n */\nfunction formatDate(date: Date, granularity: Granularity): string {\n padding to viewport to show labels near edges\n  const padding = getDaysPerUnit(granularity) * pxPerDay * 2;\n  const startDayOffset = worldXToDayOffset(viewportLeftX - padding);\n  const endDayOffset = worldXToDayOffset(viewportRightX + padding);\n\n  // Convert to dates\n  const startDate = addDays(START_DATE, startDayOffset);\n  const endDate = addDays(START_DATE, endDayOffset);\n\n  // Find the first unit boundary at or before startDate\n  let currentDate = getUnitStart(startDate, granularity);\n\n  // If currentDate is before START_DATE, advance to START_DATE\n  if (currentDate < START_DATE) {\n    currentDate = getUnitStart(START_DATE, granularity);\n    if (currentDate < START_DATE) {\n      currentDate = advanceUnit(currentDate, granularity);\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Generate labels until we pass endDate\n  while (currentDate <= endDate) {\n    const dayOffset = Math.round(\n      (currentDate.getTime() - START_DATE.getTime()) / (1000 * 60 * 60 * 24)\n    );\n    const train\n      const tasksPerYear = 8 + Math.floor(Math.random() * 5);\n\n      for (let t = 0; t < tasksPerYear; t++) {\n        // First task of year 0 for each train starts early (within first 60 Math.floor(Math.random() * 30);\n        } else {\n          dayOffset = year * 365 + Math.floor(Math.random() * 360);\n        }\n        const duration = 2 + Math.floor(Math.random() * 10);\n        const startDate = addDays(meta.startDate, dayOffset);\n        const endDate = addDays(startDate, duration);\n\n        tasks.push({\n          id: `${train.id}-${year}-${t}`,\n          trainId: train.id,\n          startDate: formatISO(startDate),\n          endDate: formatISO(endDate),\n          type: types[Math.floor(Math.random() * types.length)],\n          hasConflict: Math.random() < 0.05, // 5% conflict rate\n        });\n      }\n    }\n  });\n\n  // Add dependencies between tasks (F023)\n  // ~10-15% of tasks will have a predecessor\n  // Dependencies can be within the same train or across different trains\n  addDependencies(tasks, meta);\n\n  return tasks;\n}\n\n/**\n * Add dependency relationships between tasks (F023)\n *\n * Strategy:\n * - ~10-15% of tasks get a predecessor\n * - Predecessor must end before the current task starts (valid dependency)\n * - Mix of same-train and cross-train dependencies\n * - Overhauls often depend on inspections (realistic pattern)\n */\nfunction addDependencies(tasks: Task[], meta: DatasetMeta): void {\n  // Build a lookup by start date for efficient predecessor search\n  const sortedTasks = [...tasks].sort(\n    (a, b) => new Date(a.startDate).getTime() - new Date(b.startDate).getTime()\n  );\n\n  // Create a map for quick task lookup\n  const taskMap = new Map<string, Task>(tasks.map((t) => [t.id, t]));\n\n  // Group tasks by train for same-train dependencies\n  const tasksByTrain = new Map<string, Task[]>();\n  for (const task of sortedTasks) {\n    const list = tasksByTrain.get(task.trainId) || [];\n    list.push(task);\n    tasksByTrain.set(task.trainId, list);\n  }\n\n  // Assign dependencies\n  for (const task of tasks) {\n    // Skip if already has conflict (to avoid visual clutter)\n    if (task.hasConflict) continue;\n\n    // ~12% chance of having a dependency\n    if (Math.random() >= 0.12) continue;\n\n    const taskStartTime = new Date(task.startDate).getTime();\n\n    // 70% same-train dependency, 30% cross-train dependency\n    const sameTrain = Math.random() < 0.7;\n\n    let candidates: Task[];\n    if (sameTrain) {\n      // Find tasks on the same train that end before this one starts\n      candidates = (tasksByTrain.get(task.trainId) || []).filter((t) => {\n        const endTime = new Date(t.endDate).getTime();\n        // Predecessor must end at least 1 day before (gap for realism)\n        // and within 60 days (reasonable dependency window)\n        const gap = (taskStartTime - endTime) / (1000 * 60 * 60 * 24);\n        return t.id !== task.id && gap >= 1 && gap <= 60;\n      });\n    } else {\n      // Find tasks on different trains that end before this one starts\n      candidates = sortedTasks.filter((t) => {\n        if (t.trainId === task.trainId || t.id === task.id) return false;\n        const endTime = new Date(t.endDate).getTime();\n        const gap = (taskStartTime - endTime) / (1000 * 60 * 60 * 24);\n        return gap >= 1 && gap <= 30; // Tighter window for cross-train\n      });\n    }\n\n    if (candidates.length > 0) {\n      // Pick a random predecessor from candidates\n      const predecessor = candidates[Math.floor(Math.random() * candidates.length)];\n      task.predecessorId = predecessor.id;\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Generate complete dataset\n */\nexport function generateData(meta: DatasetMeta = DEFAULT_DATASET_META): GeneratedData {\n  const trains = generateTrains(meta.trainCount);\n  const tasks = generateTasks(trains, meta);\n\n  return { trains, tasks, meta };\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// RENDER DATA PREPARATION\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/**\n * Transform persistent data to render-ready format\n *\n * Converts:\n * - trainId → trainIndex (Y position)\n * - startDate/endDate → startDay/duration (X position and width)\n * - type → color\n */\nexport function prepareRenderData(data: GeneratedData): {\n  trains: Train[];\n  tasks: TaskRender[];\n} {\n  // Create train index lookup map\n  const trainIndexMap = new Map<string, number>(\n    data.trains.map((t, i) => [t.id, i])\n  );\n\n  // Transform tasks to render format\n  const tasks: TaskRender[] = data.tasks.map((task) => {\n    const trainIndex = trainIndexMap.get(task.trainId);\n    if (trainIndex === undefined) {\n      throw new Error(`Train not found: ${task.trainId}`);\n    }\n\n    const startDay = daysBetween(data.meta.startDate, task.startDate);\n    const duration = daysBetween(task.startDate, task.endDate);\n\n    return {\n      id: task.id,\n      trainIndex,\n      startDay,\n      duration,\n      color: TYPE_TO_COLOR[task.type],\n      hasConflict: task.hasConflict,\n      type: task.type,\n      predecessorId: task.predecessorId, // F023: dependency lines\n    };\n  });\n\n  return { trains: data.trains, tasks };\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// PRE-GENERATED DATA (for immediate use)\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n// Generate data once at module load for consistent results\nconst _generatedData = generateData();\nconst _renderData = prepareRenderData(_generatedData);\n\n/**\n * Get pre-generated dataset (persistent format)\n */\nexport function getData(): GeneratedData {\n  return _generatedData;\n}\n\n/**\n * Get pre-generated render data (canvas-ready format)\n */\nexport function getRenderData(): { trains: Train[]; tasks: TaskRender[] } {\n  return _renderData;\n}\n\n/**\n * Get just the trains array\n */\nexport function getTrains(): Train[] {\n  return _generatedData.trains;\n}\n\n/**\n * Get just the render tasks array\n */\nexport function getTasks(): TaskRender[] {\n  return _renderData.tasks;\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":121,"oldLines":15,"newStart":121,"newLines":12,"lines":["  * - Mix of 'train' : 'tram';\n}\n\n/**\n * Format date for display (e.g., \"Mar 15, 2024\")\n */\nfunction formatDate(isoDate: string): string {\n  const date = new Date(isoDate);\n  return date.toLocaleDateString('en-US', {\n    weekday: 'short',\n    month: 'short',\n    day: 'numeric',\n    year: 'numeric',\n  });\n}\n\n/**\n * Calculate duration in days from ISO dates\n */\nfunction Math.round(diffTime / (1000 * 60 * 60 * 24));\n}\n\n/**\n * Capitalize first letter\n */\nfunction capitalize(str: string): string {\n  return str.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + str.slice(1);\n}\n\n// );\n\n    return {\n      id: task.id,\n      trainId: task.trainId,\n      trainName: train?.name || task.trainId,\n      assetType: train?.assetType || 'ALSTOM',\n      type: task.type,\n - start.getTime();\n  return Math.round(diffTime / (1000 * 60 * 60 * 24));\n}\n\n/**\n * Capitalize first letter\n */\nfunction capitalize(str: string): string {\n  return str.charAt(0).toUpperCase() * Internal component that reads r3f-perf data and logs it\n * Must be rendered inside R3F Canvas context\n */\nfunction PerfLogger({ currentEvent }: { currentEvent: PerfEvent }) {\n  const perfData = number;\n    gpu?: number | null;\n    calls?: number;\n    triangles?: number;\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Internal component that reads r3f-perf data and logs it\n * Must be rendered inside R3F Canvas context\n */\nfunction PerfLogger({ currentEvent }: { currentEvent: PerfEvent }) {\n  const perfData = usePerf() as PerfDataWithLog;\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    if (!perfData) return;\n\n    frameCounter++;\n\n    // Track FPS for rolling average\n    const currentFps = perfData.log?.fps ?? 0;\n    const currentMs = perfData.log?.ms ?? 16.67;","originalFile":"/**\n * PerfMonitor - Performance monitoring component for WebGL/R3F applications\n *\n * Features:\n * - Real-time FPS, MS, Memory, GPU, Draw Calls, Triangles display via r3f-perf\n * - Detailed logging system with circular buffer (60s of metrics)\n * - Session recording for performance testing\n * - Global API exposed via window for programmatic access\n *\n * Usage:\n * - Visual: Press 'P' key or use toggle button to show/hide panel\n * - Console: window.__PERF_LOG__ - access log buffer\n * - Console: window.__PERF_EXPORT__() - export full log as array\n * - Console: window.__PERF_START_SESSION__('test') - start recording session\n * - Console: window.__PERF_STOP_SESSION__() - stop and get session stats\n *\n * @see docs/PERFORMANCE_LOGGING.md for detailed documentation\n */\n\nimport { useEffect, useCallback, useState } from 'react';\nimport { Perf, usePerf } from 'r3f-perf';\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// TYPES\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/**\n * Event type for performance logging\n */\ntype PerfEvent = 'idle' | 'panning' | 'zooming' | 'animating' | 'lod_transition' | 'unknown';\n\n/**\n * Single metric entry in the log buffer\n */\ninterface PerfLogEntry {\n  timestamp: number;\n  fps: {\n    current: number;\n    avg: number;\n    min: number;\n    max: number;\n  };\n  ms: {\n    current: number;\n    avg: number;\n    max: number;\n  };\n  memory: number; // MB\n  gpu: number | null; // GPU time in ms (may not be available)\n  drawCalls: number;\n  triangles: number;\n  event: PerfEvent;\n}\n\n/**\n * Session statistics returned by __PERF_STOP_SESSION__\n */\ninterface PerfSessionStats {\n  name: string;\n  duration: number; // seconds\n  totalFrames: number;\n  fps: {\n    avg: number;\n    min: number;\n    max: number;\n    p1: number; // 1st percentile (worst 1%)\n    p99: number; // 99th percentile (best 99%)\n  };\n  ms: {\n    avg: number;\n    max: number;\n  };\n  framesBelow30: number;\n  framesBelow60: number;\n  entries: PerfLogEntry[];\n}\n\n/**\n * Active session state\n */\ninterface PerfSession {\n  name: string;\n  startTime: number;\n  entries: PerfLogEntry[];\n  fpsValues: number[];\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// CONSTANTS\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nconst LOG_BUFFER_DURATION_MS = 60000; // 60 seconds of logs\nconst LOG_INTERVAL_FRAMES = 60; // Log every 60 frames (~1 second at 60fps)\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// GLOBAL STATE (accessible via window)\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n// Circular buffer for performance logs\nlet perfLogBuffer: PerfLogEntry[] = [];\nlet currentSession: PerfSession | null = null;\nlet frameCounter = 0;\nlet fpsHistory: number[] = [];\nlet msHistory: number[] = [];\n\n// Rolling statistics for averaging\nconst rollingWindow = 60; // frames for rolling average\n\n/**\n * Clear old entries from buffer based on timestamp\n */\nfunction pruneOldEntries() {\n  const cutoff = Date.now() - LOG_BUFFER_DURATION_MS;\n  perfLogBuffer = perfLogBuffer.filter((entry) => entry.timestamp > cutoff);\n}\n\n/**\n * Calculate percentile from sorted array\n */\nfunction percentile(sortedArr: number[], p: number): number {\n  if (sortedArr.length === 0) return 0;\n  const index = Math.ceil((p / 100) * sortedArr.length) - 1;\n  return sortedArr[Math.max(0, Math.min(index, sortedArr.length - 1))];\n}\n\n/**\n * Export the global performance API to window\n */\nfunction setupGlobalAPI() {\n  // Make log buffer accessible\n  Object.defineProperty(window, '__PERF_LOG__', {\n    get: () => [...perfLogBuffer],\n    configurable: true,\n  });\n\n  // Export full log\n  (window as any).__PERF_EXPORT__ = (): PerfLogEntry[] => {\n    return [...perfLogBuffer];\n  };\n\n  // Start a named session\n  (window as any).__PERF_START_SESSION__ = (name: string): void => {\n    if (currentSession) {\n      console.warn(`[PERF] Session \"${currentSession.name}\" already running. Stopping it first.`);\n      (window as any).__PERF_STOP_SESSION__();\n    }\n    currentSession = {\n      name,\n      startTime: Date.now(),\n      entries: [],\n      fpsValues: [],\n    };\n    console.log(`[PERF] Session \"${name}\" started`);\n  };\n\n  // Stop current session and return stats\n  (window as any).__PERF_STOP_SESSION__ = (): PerfSessionStats | null => {\n    if (!currentSession) {\n      console.warn('[PERF] No active session to stop');\n      return null;\n    }\n\n    const session = currentSession;\n    currentSession = null;\n\n    const duration = (Date.now() - session.startTime) / 1000;\n    const sortedFps = [...session.fpsValues].sort((a, b) => a - b);\n\n    const stats: PerfSessionStats = {\n      name: session.name,\n      duration,\n      totalFrames: session.fpsValues.length,\n      fps: {\n        avg: sortedFps.length > 0 ? sortedFps.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / sortedFps.length : 0,\n        min: sortedFps.length > 0 ? sortedFps[0] : 0,\n        max: sortedFps.length > 0 ? sortedFps[sortedFps.length - 1] : 0,\n        p1: percentile(sortedFps, 1),\n        p99: percentile(sortedFps, 99),\n      },\n      ms: {\n        avg: session.entries.length > 0\n          ? session.entries.reduce((a, e) => a + e.ms.current, 0) / session.entries.length\n          : 0,\n        max: session.entries.length > 0\n          ? Math.max(...session.entries.map((e) => e.ms.current))\n          : 0,\n      },\n      framesBelow30: session.fpsValues.filter((fps) => fps < 30).length,\n      framesBelow60: session.fpsValues.filter((fps) => fps < 60).length,\n      entries: session.entries,\n    };\n\n    console.log(`[PERF] Session \"${session.name}\" stopped:`, {\n      duration: `${duration.toFixed(2)}s`,\n      avgFPS: stats.fps.avg.toFixed(1),\n      p1FPS: stats.fps.p1.toFixed(1),\n      p99FPS: stats.fps.p99.toFixed(1),\n      framesBelow30: stats.framesBelow30,\n      framesBelow60: stats.framesBelow60,\n    });\n\n    return stats;\n  };\n\n  // Get current session status\n  (window as any).__PERF_SESSION_STATUS__ = (): { active: boolean; name?: string; duration?: number } => {\n    if (!currentSession) {\n      return { active: false };\n    }\n    return {\n      active: true,\n      name: currentSession.name,\n      duration: (Date.now() - currentSession.startTime) / 1000,\n    };\n  };\n\n  // Clear log buffer\n  (window as any).__PERF_CLEAR__ = (): void => {\n    perfLogBuffer = [];\n    console.log('[PERF] Log buffer cleared');\n  };\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// PERF LOGGER COMPONENT (runs inside R3F Canvas)\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/**\n * Internal component that reads r3f-perf data and logs it\n * Must be rendered inside R3F Canvas context\n */\nfunction PerfLogger({ currentEvent }: { currentEvent: PerfEvent }) {\n  const perfData = usePerf();\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    if (!perfData) return;\n\n    frameCounter++;\n\n    // Track FPS for rolling average\n    const currentFps = perfData.log?.fps ?? 0;\n    const currentMs = perfData.log?.ms ?? 16.67;\n\n    fpsHistory.push(currentFps);\n    msHistory.push(currentMs);\n\n    // Keep rolling window\n    if (fpsHistory.length > rollingWindow) {\n      fpsHistory.shift();\n      msHistory.shift();\n    }\n\n    // Track for active session (every frame)\n    if (currentSession) {\n      currentSession.fpsValues.push(currentFps);\n    }\n\n    // Log every N frames\n    if (frameCounter >= LOG_INTERVAL_FRAMES) {\n      frameCounter = 0;\n\n      // Calculate rolling stats\n      const avgFps = fpsHistory.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / fpsHistory.length;\n      const minFps = Math.min(...fpsHistory);\n      const maxFps = Math.max(...fpsHistory);\n      const avgMs = msHistory.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / msHistory.length;\n      const maxMs = Math.max(...msHistory);\n\n      const entry: PerfLogEntry = {\n        timestamp: Date.now(),\n        fps: {\n          current: currentFps,\n          avg: avgFps,\n          min: minFps,\n          max: maxFps,\n        },\n        ms: {\n          current: currentMs,\n          avg: avgMs,\n          max: maxMs,\n        },\n        memory: perfData.log?.mem ?? 0,\n        gpu: perfData.log?.gpu ?? null,\n        drawCalls: perfData.log?.calls ?? 0,\n        triangles: perfData.log?.triangles ?? 0,\n        event: currentEvent,\n      };\n\n      // Add to buffer\n      perfLogBuffer.push(entry);\n      pruneOldEntries();\n\n      // Add to active session\n      if (currentSession) {\n        currentSession.entries.push(entry);\n      }\n    }\n  });\n\n  return null;\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// MAIN PERF MONITOR COMPONENT\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\ninterface PerfMonitorProps {\n  /** Whether the perf panel is visible */\n  visible?: boolean;\n  /** Callback when visibility changes (for external control) */\n  onVisibilityChange?: (visible: boolean) => void;\n  /** Current event type for logging context */\n  currentEvent?: PerfEvent;\n  /** Position of the panel */\n  position?: 'top-left' | 'top-right' | 'bottom-left' | 'bottom-right';\n}\n\n/**\n * Performance monitor component with r3f-perf panel and logging system\n *\n * This component should be rendered INSIDE the R3F Canvas.\n *\n * @example\n * <Canvas>\n *   <PerfMonitor visible={showPerf} />\n *   {... other scene content ...}\n * </Canvas>\n */\nexport function PerfMonitor({\n  visible = true,\n  currentEvent = 'idle',\n  position = 'bottom-left', // BUG-006: Changed from top-right to avoid blocking navbar controls\n}: PerfMonitorProps) {\n  // Setup global API on mount\n  useEffect(() => {\n    setupGlobalAPI();\n    console.log('[PERF] Performance monitor initialized. Available commands:');\n    console.log('  window.__PERF_LOG__ - Access log buffer');\n    console.log('  window.__PERF_EXPORT__() - Export full log');\n    console.log('  window.__PERF_START_SESSION__(name) - Start recording');\n    console.log('  window.__PERF_STOP_SESSION__() - Stop and get stats');\n    console.log('  window.__PERF_CLEAR__() - Clear log buffer');\n\n    return () => {\n      // Cleanup (optional - keep API available for debugging)\n    };\n  }, []);\n\n  return (\n    <>\n      {/* r3f-perf visual panel */}\n      {visible && (\n        <Perf\n          position={position}\n          minimal={false}\n          showGraph={true}\n          colorBlind={false}\n          antialias={true}\n          overClock={true}\n          deepAnalyze={true}\n          matrixUpdate={true}\n        />\n      )}\n\n      {/* Logger that runs continuously (even when panel is hidden) */}\n      <PerfLogger currentEvent={currentEvent} />\n    </>\n  );\n}\n\n/**\n * Hook to toggle perf monitor visibility with keyboard shortcut\n *\n * @example\n * const { visible, toggle } = usePerfMonitorToggle();\n */\nexport function usePerfMonitorToggle(initialVisible = false) {\n  const [visible, setVisible] = useState(initialVisible);\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    const handleKeyDown = (e: KeyboardEvent) => {\n      // Toggle on 'P' key (not when typing in input)\n      if (\n        e.key === 'p' &&\n        !e.ctrlKey &&\n        !e.metaKey &&\n        !e.altKey &&\n        !(e.target instanceof HTMLInputElement) &&\n        !(e.target instanceof HTMLTextAreaElement)\n      ) {\n        setVisible((v) => !v);\n      }\n    };\n\n    window.addEventListener('keydown', handleKeyDown);\n    return () => window.removeEventListener('keydown', handleKeyDown);\n  }, []);\n\n  const toggle = useCallback(() => setVisible((v) => !v), []);\n\n  return { visible, toggle, setVisible };\n}\n\n// Export types
