TimelineHeader - Displays date labels synced with canvas panX\n *\n * Features:\n * - Adaptive granularity based on zoom level:\n *   - Low zoom (< 0.5): Years\n *   - Medium zoom (0.5-1.5): Months\n *   - High zoom (1.5-3.0): Weeks\n *   - Very high zoom (> 3.0): Days\n * - Labels positioned to align with canvas content\n * - Labels don't overlap (spacing adjusted based on zoom)\n *\n * D3-MIGRATE-001: Uses d3-time and d3-time-format for date operations\n */\n\nimport { useMemo } from 'react';\nimport { timeYear, timeMonth, timeWeek, timeDay } from 'd3-time';\nimport { timeFormat } from 'd3-time-format';\nimport { DEFAULT_VIEW_CONFIG, DEFAULT_DATASET_META } from '../data/types';\n\ninterface TimelineHeaderProps {\n  /** Left edge of the visible viewport in world X coordinates */\n  viewportLeftX: number;\n  /** Right edge of the visible viewport in world X coordinates */\n  viewportRightX: number;\n  /** Current zoom level */\n  zoom: number;\n  /** Width of the sidebar (to offset the timeline) */\n  sidebarWidth: number;\n  /** Callback when a date label is clicked (F124: navigation) */\n  onDateClick?: (dayOffset: number) => void;\n}\n\nconst { pxPerDay } = DEFAULT_VIEW_CONFIG;\nconst START_DATE = new Date(DEFAULT_DATASET_META.startDate);\n\n// D3-MIGRATE-001: Format helpers using d3-time-format\nconst formatYear = timeFormat('%Y');\nconst formatMonth = timeFormat('%b %Y');\nconst formatWeek = timeFormat('%b %d');\nconst formatDay = timeFormat('%a, %b %d');\n\n/**\n * Calculate the appropriate granularity based on zoom level\n */\ntype Granularity = 'year' | 'month' | 'week' | 'day';\n\nfunction getGranularity(zoom: number): Granularity {\n  if (zoom < 0.5) return 'year';\n  if (zoom < 1.5) return 'month';\n  if (zoom < 3.0) return 'week';\n  return 'day';\n}\n\n/**\n * Get the minimum pixel spacing between labels for each granularity\n * Note: Currently unused but kept for future label spacing optimization\n */\nfunction _getMinLabelSpacing(granularity: Granularity): number {\n  switch (granularity) {\n    case 'year':\n      return 80;\n    case 'month':\n      return 70;\n    case 'week':\n      return 60;\n    case 'day':\n      return 50;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Convert world X coordinate to day offset from start date\n */\nfunction worldXToDayOffset(worldX: number): number {\n  return Math.floor(worldX / pxPerDay);\n}\n\n/**\n * Convert day offset to world X coordinate\n */\nfunction dayOffsetToWorldX(dayOffset: number): number {\n  return dayOffset * pxPerDay;\n}\n\n// D3-MIGRATE-001: Time interval map for granularity-based operations\nconst timeIntervals = {\n  year: timeYear,\n  month: timeMonth,\n  week: timeWeek,\n  day: timeDay,\n} as const;\n\n/**\n * Add days to a date (D3-MIGRATE-001: uses d3-time)\n */\nfunction addDays(date: Date, days: number): Date {\n  return timeDay.offset(date, days);\n}\n\n/**\n * Get the start of a time unit (D3-MIGRATE-001: uses d3-time floor)\n */\nfunction getUnitStart(date: Date, granularity: Granularity): Date {\n  return timeIntervals[granularity].floor(date);\n}\n\n/**\n * Get days until next unit\n */\nfunction getDaysPerUnit(granularity: Granularity): number {\n  switch (granularity) {\n    case 'year':\n      return 365;\n    case 'month':\n      return 30;\n    case 'week':\n      return 7;\n    case 'day':\n      return 1;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Advance to next unit (D3-MIGRATE-001: uses d3-time offset)\n */\nfunction advanceUnit(date: Date, granularity: Granularity): Date {\n  return timeIntervals[granularity].offset(date, 1);\n}\n\n/**\n * Format date based on granularity\n */\nfunction formatDate(date: Date, granularity: Granularity): string {\n  switch (granularity) {\n    case 'year':\n      return formatYear(date);\n    case 'month':\n      return formatMonth(date);\n    case 'week':\n      return formatWeek(date);\n    case 'day':\n      return formatDay(date);\n  }\n}\n\ninterface TimeLabel {\n  dayOffset: number;\n  worldX: number;\n  label: string;\n  isMinor: boolean; // For smaller tick marks\n}\n\n/**\n * Generate labels for the visible viewport\n */\nfunction generateLabels(\n  viewportLeftX: number,\n  viewportRightX: number,\n  granularity: Granularity\n): TimeLabel[] {\n  const labels: TimeLabel[] = [];\n\n  // Add padding to viewport to show labels near edges\n  const padding = getDaysPerUnit(granularity) * pxPerDay * 2;\n  const startDayOffset = worldXToDayOffset(viewportLeftX - padding);\n  const endDayOffset = worldXToDayOffset(viewportRightX + padding);\n\n  // Convert to dates\n  const startDate = addDays(START_DATE, startDayOffset);\n  const endDate = addDays(START_DATE, endDayOffset);\n\n  // Find the first unit boundary at or before startDate\n  let currentDate = getUnitStart(startDate, granularity);\n\n  // If currentDate is before START_DATE, advance to START_DATE\n  if (currentDate < START_DATE) {\n    currentDate = getUnitStart(START_DATE, granularity);\n    if (currentDate < START_DATE) {\n      currentDate = advanceUnit(currentDate, granularity);\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Generate labels until we pass endDate\n  while (currentDate <= endDate) {\n    const dayOffset = Math.round(\n      (currentDate.getTime() - START_DATE.getTime()) / (1000 * 60 * 60 * 24)\n    );\n    const worldX = dayOffsetToWorldX(dayOffset);\n\n    // Only add labels within reasonable range\n    if (worldX >= viewportLeftX - padding && worldX <= viewportRightX + padding) {\n      labels.push({\n        dayOffset,\n        worldX,\n        label: formatDate(currentDate, granularity),\n        isMinor: false,\n      });\n    }\n\n    currentDate = advanceUnit(currentDate, granularity);\n  }\n\n  return labels;\n}\n\nexport function TimelineHeader({\n  viewportLeftX,\n  viewportRightX,\n  zoom,\n  sidebarWidth,\n  onDateClick,\n}: TimelineHeaderProps) {\n  const granularity = useMemo(() => getGranularity(zoom), [zoom]);\n\n  const labels = useMemo(\n    () => generateLabels(viewportLeftX, viewportRightX, granularity),\n    [viewportLeftX, viewportRightX, granularity]\n  );\n\n  // Calculate viewport width with defensive check\n  const viewportWidth = viewportRightX - viewportLeftX;\n\n  // BUG-005: Guard against invalid viewport (e.g., during initialization)\n  const isViewportValid = isFinite(viewportWidth) && viewportWidth > 0;\n\n  return (\n    <div\n      style={{\n        height: 32,\n        backgroundColor: '#f5f8fa',\n        borderBottom: '1px solid #e1e8ed',\n        display: 'flex',\n        alignItems: 'center',\n        overflow: 'hidden',\n        position: 'relative',\n        marginLeft: sidebarWidth,\n      }}\n    >\n      {isViewportValid && labels.map((label, index) => {\n        // Calculate position as percentage of viewport\n        const positionInViewport = label.worldX - viewportLeftX;\n        const percentPosition = (positionInViewport / viewportWidth) * 100;\n\n        // BUG-005: Skip labels that would be too far off screen\n        // Use wider bounds for year granularity since labels are spaced far apart\n        const skipMargin = granularity === 'year' ? 50 : 20;\n        if (!isFinite(percentPosition) || percentPosition < -skipMargin || percentPosition > 100 + skipMargin) {\n          return null;\n        }\n\n        // BUG-005: Adjust transform for edge labels to keep them visible\n        // At edges, don't center the label - align it to stay within bounds\n        let transformX = '-50%';  // Default: center on position\n        if (percentPosition < 5) {\n          // Near left edge: align left edge to position\n          transformX = '0%';\n        } else if (percentPosition > 95) {\n          // Near right edge: align right edge to position\n          transformX = '-100%';\n        }\n\n        return (\n          <div\n            key={`${label.label}-${index}`}\n            onClick={() => onDateClick?.(label.dayOffset)}\n            style={{\n              position: 'absolute',\n              left: `${percentPosition}%`,\n              transform: `translateX(${transformX})`,\n              fontSize: 11,\n              fontFamily: 'monospace',\n              color: '#5c7080',\n              whiteSpace: 'nowrap',\n TECH-DEBT-002: r3f-perf usePerf hook signature may require argument\n  const perfData = (usePerf as any)() as PerfDataWithLog;","originalFile":"/**\n * PerfMonitor - Performance monitoring component for WebGL/R3F applications\n *\n * Features:\n * - Real-time FPS, MS, Memory, GPU, Draw Calls, Triangles display via r3f-perf\n * - Detailed logging system with circular buffer (60s of metrics)\n * - Session recording for performance testing\n * - Global API exposed via window for programmatic access\n *\n * Usage:\n * - Visual: Press 'P' key or use toggle button to show/hide panel\n * - Console: window.__PERF_LOG__ - access log buffer\n * - Console: window.__PERF_EXPORT__() - export full log as array\n * - Console: window.__PERF_START_SESSION__('test') - start recording session\n * - Console: window.__PERF_STOP_SESSION__() - stop and get session stats\n *\n * @see docs/PERFORMANCE_LOGGING.md for detailed documentation\n */\n\nimport { useEffect, useCallback, useState } from 'react';\nimport { Perf, usePerf } from 'r3f-perf';\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// TYPES\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/**\n * Event type for performance logging\n */\ntype PerfEvent = 'idle' | 'panning' | 'zooming' | 'animating' | 'lod_transition' | 'unknown';\n\n/**\n * Single metric entry in the log buffer\n */\ninterface PerfLogEntry {\n  timestamp: number;\n  fps: {\n    current: number;\n    avg: number;\n    min: number;\n    max: number;\n  };\n  ms: {\n    current: number;\n    avg: number;\n    max: number;\n  };\n  memory: number; // MB\n  gpu: number | null; // GPU time in ms (may not be available)\n  drawCalls: number;\n  triangles: number;\n  event: PerfEvent;\n}\n\n/**\n * Session statistics returned by __PERF_STOP_SESSION__\n */\ninterface PerfSessionStats {\n  name: string;\n  duration: number; // seconds\n  totalFrames: number;\n  fps: {\n    avg: number;\n    min: number;\n    max: number;\n    p1: number; // 1st percentile (worst 1%)\n    p99: number; // 99th percentile (best 99%)\n  };\n  ms: {\n    avg: number;\n    max: number;\n  };\n  framesBelow30: number;\n  framesBelow60: number;\n  entries: PerfLogEntry[];\n}\n\n/**\n * Active session state\n */\ninterface PerfSession {\n  name: string;\n  startTime: number;\n  entries: PerfLogEntry[];\n  fpsValues: number[];\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// CONSTANTS\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\nconst LOG_BUFFER_DURATION_MS = 60000; // 60 seconds of logs\nconst LOG_INTERVAL_FRAMES = 60; // Log every 60 frames (~1 second at 60fps)\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// GLOBAL STATE (accessible via window)\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n// Circular buffer for performance logs\nlet perfLogBuffer: PerfLogEntry[] = [];\nlet currentSession: PerfSession | null = null;\nlet frameCounter = 0;\nlet fpsHistory: number[] = [];\nlet msHistory: number[] = [];\n\n// Rolling statistics for averaging\nconst rollingWindow = 60; // frames for rolling average\n\n/**\n * Clear old entries from buffer based on timestamp\n */\nfunction pruneOldEntries() {\n  const cutoff = Date.now() - LOG_BUFFER_DURATION_MS;\n  perfLogBuffer = perfLogBuffer.filter((entry) => entry.timestamp > cutoff);\n}\n\n/**\n * Calculate percentile from sorted array\n */\nfunction percentile(sortedArr: number[], p: number): number {\n  if (sortedArr.length === 0) return 0;\n  const index = Math.ceil((p / 100) * sortedArr.length) - 1;\n  return sortedArr[Math.max(0, Math.min(index, sortedArr.length - 1))];\n}\n\n/**\n * Export the global performance API to window\n */\nfunction setupGlobalAPI() {\n  // Make log buffer accessible\n  Object.defineProperty(window, '__PERF_LOG__', {\n    get: () => [...perfLogBuffer],\n    configurable: true,\n  });\n\n  // Export full log\n  (window as any).__PERF_EXPORT__ = (): PerfLogEntry[] => {\n    return [...perfLogBuffer];\n  };\n\n  // Start a named session\n  (window as any).__PERF_START_SESSION__ = (name: string): void => {\n    if (currentSession) {\n      console.warn(`[PERF] Session \"${currentSession.name}\" already running. Stopping it first.`);\n      (window as any).__PERF_STOP_SESSION__();\n    }\n    currentSession = {\n      name,\n      startTime: Date.now(),\n      entries: [],\n      fpsValues: [],\n    };\n    console.log(`[PERF] Session \"${name}\" started`);\n  };\n\n  // Stop current session and return stats\n  (window as any).__PERF_STOP_SESSION__ = (): PerfSessionStats | null => {\n    if (!currentSession) {\n      console.warn('[PERF] No active session to stop');\n      return null;\n    }\n\n    const session = currentSession;\n    currentSession = null;\n\n    const duration = (Date.now() - session.startTime) / 1000;\n    const sortedFps = [...session.fpsValues].sort((a, b) => a - b);\n\n    const stats: PerfSessionStats = {\n      name: session.name,\n      duration,\n      totalFrames: session.fpsValues.length,\n      fps: {\n        avg: sortedFps.length > 0 ? sortedFps.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / sortedFps.length : 0,\n        min: sortedFps.length > 0 ? sortedFps[0] : 0,\n        max: sortedFps.length > 0 ? sortedFps[sortedFps.length - 1] : 0,\n        p1: percentile(sortedFps, 1),\n        p99: percentile(sortedFps, 99),\n      },\n      ms: {\n        avg: session.entries.length > 0\n          ? session.entries.reduce((a, e) => a + e.ms.current, 0) / session.entries.length\n          : 0,\n        max: session.entries.length > 0\n          ? Math.max(...session.entries.map((e) => e.ms.current))\n          : 0,\n      },\n      framesBelow30: session.fpsValues.filter((fps) => fps < 30).length,\n      framesBelow60: session.fpsValues.filter((fps) => fps < 60).length,\n      entries: session.entries,\n    };\n\n    console.log(`[PERF] Session \"${session.name}\" stopped:`, {\n      duration: `${duration.toFixed(2)}s`,\n      avgFPS: stats.fps.avg.toFixed(1),\n      p1FPS: stats.fps.p1.toFixed(1),\n      p99FPS: stats.fps.p99.toFixed(1),\n      framesBelow30: stats.framesBelow30,\n      framesBelow60: stats.framesBelow60,\n    });\n\n    return stats;\n  };\n\n  // Get current session status\n  (window as any).__PERF_SESSION_STATUS__ = (): { active: boolean; name?: string; duration?: number } => {\n    if (!currentSession) {\n      return { active: false };\n    }\n    return {\n      active: true,\n      name: currentSession.name,\n      duration: (Date.now() - currentSession.startTime) / 1000,\n    };\n  };\n\n  // Clear log buffer\n  (window as any).__PERF_CLEAR__ = (): void => {\n    perfLogBuffer = [];\n    console.log('[PERF] Log buffer cleared');\n  };\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// PERF LOGGER COMPONENT (runs inside R3F Canvas)\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n// TECH-DEBT-002: r3f-perf types don't expose log property correctly\ninterface PerfDataWithLog {\n  log?: {\n    fps?: number;\n    ms?: number;\n    mem?: number;\n    gpu?: number | null;\n    calls?: number;\n    triangles?: number;\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Internal component that reads r3f-perf data and logs it\n * Must be rendered inside R3F Canvas context\n */\nfunction PerfLogger({ currentEvent }: { currentEvent: PerfEvent }) {\n  const perfData = usePerf() as PerfDataWithLog;\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    if (!perfData) return;\n\n    frameCounter++;\n\n    // Track FPS for rolling average\n    const currentFps = perfData.log?.fps ?? 0;\n    const currentMs = perfData.log?.ms ?? 16.67;\n\n    fpsHistory.push(currentFps);\n    msHistory.push(currentMs);\n\n    // Keep rolling window\n    if (fpsHistory.length > rollingWindow) {\n      fpsHistory.shift();\n      msHistory.shift();\n    }\n\n    // Track for active session (every frame)\n    if (currentSession) {\n      currentSession.fpsValues.push(currentFps);\n    }\n\n    // Log every N frames\n    if (frameCounter >= LOG_INTERVAL_FRAMES) {\n      frameCounter = 0;\n\n      // Calculate rolling stats\n      const avgFps = fpsHistory.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / fpsHistory.length;\n      const minFps = Math.min(...fpsHistory);\n      const maxFps = Math.max(...fpsHistory);\n      const avgMs = msHistory.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / msHistory.length;\n      const maxMs = Math.max(...msHistory);\n\n      const entry: PerfLogEntry = {\n        timestamp: Date.now(),\n        fps: {\n          current: currentFps,\n          avg: avgFps,\n          min: minFps,\n          max: maxFps,\n        },\n        ms: {\n          current: currentMs,\n          avg: avgMs,\n          max: maxMs,\n        },\n        memory: perfData.log?.mem ?? 0,\n        gpu: perfData.log?.gpu ?? null,\n        drawCalls: perfData.log?.calls ?? 0,\n        triangles: perfData.log?.triangles ?? 0,\n        event: currentEvent,\n      };\n\n      // Add to buffer\n      perfLogBuffer.push(entry);\n      pruneOldEntries();\n\n      // Add to active session\n      if (currentSession) {\n        currentSession.entries.push(entry);\n      }\n    }\n  });\n\n  return null;\n}\n\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// MAIN PERF MONITOR COMPONENT\n// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\ninterface PerfMonitorProps {\n  /** Whether the perf panel is visible */\n  visible?: boolean;\n  /** Callback when visibility changes (for external control) */\n  onVisibilityChange?: (visible: boolean) => void;\n  /** Current event type for logging context */\n  currentEvent?: PerfEvent;\n  /** Position of the panel */\n  position?: 'top-left' | 'top-right' | 'bottom-left' | 'bottom-right';\n}\n\n/**\n * Performance monitor component with r3f-perf panel and logging system\n *\n * This component should be rendered INSIDE the R3F Canvas.\n *\n * @example\n * <Canvas>\n *   <PerfMonitor visible={showPerf} />\n *   {... other scene content ...}\n * </Canvas>\n */\nexport function PerfMonitor({\n  visible = true,\n  currentEvent = 'idle',\n  position = 'bottom-left', // BUG-006: Changed from top-right to avoid blocking navbar controls\n}: PerfMonitorProps) {\n  // Setup global API on mount\n  useEffect(() => {\n    setupGlobalAPI();\n    console.log('[PERF] Performance monitor initialized. Available commands:');\n    console.log('  window.__PERF_LOG__ - Access log buffer');\n    console.log('  window.__PERF_EXPORT__() - Export full log');\n    console.log('  window.__PERF_START_SESSION__(name) - Start recording');\n    console.log('  window.__PERF_STOP_SESSION__() - Stop and get stats');\n    console.log('  window.__PERF_CLEAR__() - Clear log buffer');\n\n    return () => {\n      // Cleanup (optional - keep API available for debugging)\n    };\n  }, []);\n\n  return (\n    <>\n      {/* r3f-perf visual panel */}\n      {visible && (\n        <Perf\n          position={position}\n          minimal={false}\n          showGraph={true}\n          colorBlind={false}\n          antialias={true}\n          overClock={true}\n          deepAnalyze={true}\n          matrixUpdate={true}\n        />\n      )}\n\n      {/* Logger that runs continuously (even when panel is hidden) */}\n      <PerfLogger currentEvent={currentEvent} />\n    </>\n  );\n}\n\n/**\n * Hook to toggle perf monitor visibility with keyboard shortcut\n *\n * @example\n * const { visible, toggle } = usePerfMonitorToggle();\n */\nexport function usePerfMonitorToggle(initialVisible = false) {\n  const [visible, setVisible] = useState(initialVisible);\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    const handleKeyDown = (e: KeyboardEvent) => {\n      // Toggle on 'P' key (not when typing in input)\n      if (\n        e.key === 'p' &&\n        !e.ctrlKey &&\n        !e.metaKey &&\n        !e.altKey &&\n        !(e.target instanceof HTMLInputElement) &&\n        !(e.target instanceof HTMLTextAreaElement)\n      ) {\n        setVisible((v) => !v);\n      }\n    };\n\n    window.addEventListener('keydown', handleKeyDown);\n    return () => window.removeEventListener('keydown', handleKeyDown);\n  }, []);\n\n  const toggle = useCallback(() => setVisible((v) => !v), []);\n\n  return { visible, toggle, setVisible };\n}\n\n// Export types for external use\nexport type { PerfEvent, PerfLogEntry, PerfSessionStats };\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":242,"oldLines":7,"newStart":242,"newLines":8,"lines":["  * Must be rendered inside R3F
